steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence states a general principle about energy transfers across trophic levels. The second sentence illustrates that principle with a specific food-chain sequence. The transition must signal a move from general concept to concrete example.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — By contrast,: Signals opposition or contradiction. The second sentence illustrates the first, not opposes it. ✗
  • Choice B — Likewise,: Signals similarity or a parallel point, but the second sentence gives an example rather than a merely similar point. ✗
  • Choice C — However,: Signals contrast or concession, but the second sentence illustrates rather than contrasts with the first. ✗
  • Choice D — For example,: Introduces a specific case that demonstrates the general principle — exactly the relationship between the two sentences. ✓
D is correct.
